summary: Daily Hacker News product scout: 12 fresh, concrete items selected from 19 fully crawled qualifying discussions.
telegram_footer: 12 items surfaced | 19/19 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Kimi K3
items.summary: An AI model for agentic coding and knowledge work, praised as a credible frontier alternative.
items.why_included: A high-signal model launch with substantive hands-on discussion rather than benchmark-only interest.
items.evidence: Commenters called its jump in coding usefulness striking, while debating price, openness, and real-world reliability.

items.name: Soofi S
items.source_story_title: German AI consortium releases Soofi S, an open 30B model that tops benchmarks
items.summary: A sovereign German-English open foundation-model family with 32B preview checkpoints.
items.why_included: A concrete European open-model release worth watching despite unresolved benchmark-comparison questions.
items.evidence: HN commenters linked the official Hugging Face project and challenged its benchmark baselines and possible contamination.

items.name: Agent-talk
items.source_story_title: Agent-talk: Enabling coding agents to work together
items.summary: A plugin and CLI for direct messaging between coding-agent sessions.
items.why_included: It targets a practical coordination gap in multi-agent coding workflows.
items.evidence: Discussion focused on trust, push delivery, harness integration, and avoiding stale-session costs.

items.name: Wire
items.source_story_title: Agent-talk: Enabling coding agents to work together
items.summary: A vendor-free agent-to-agent communication protocol with a telephone-exchange model.
items.why_included: A concrete alternative surfaced in the agent-collaboration discussion.
items.evidence: Its creator described Wire as an open-source approach to zero-knowledge trust across agent environments.

items.name: Microsoft Comic Chat
items.source_story_title: Microsoft Comic Chat is now open source
items.summary: Microsoft’s 1990s comic-book IRC client, now released as source code.
items.why_included: A historically interesting code release with active interest in modernizing and building it.
items.evidence: HN commenters supplied the official repository and discussed its IRC extensions and current-toolchain revival.

items.name: Leaves
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Leaves – A text-UI disk usage treemap visualizer
items.summary: A Rust text-mode treemap for inspecting disk usage interactively.
items.why_included: A small, direct utility that commenters wanted beside their everyday Linux tools.
items.evidence: Users compared it favorably with WinDirStat, duc, and SequoiaView while suggesting indexing improvements.

items.name: Sling
items.source_story_title: Guide to data tools landscape for developers
items.summary: An open-source Go CLI for ELT, data replication, and quality checks.
items.why_included: A concrete data-integration recommendation surfaced from a broad developer tooling guide.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended Sling as a CLI-first single Go binary for data ingestion.

items.name: Decoy Font
items.summary: A free TTF optical-illusion font that embeds two messages in each glyph.
items.why_included: A memorable creative experiment with an immediately testable download.
items.evidence: Commenters tested its AI-readability claims and argued its strongest use is visual art, not security.

items.name: Paper2Audio
items.summary: A document text-to-speech service optimized for papers, math, and complex PDFs.
items.why_included: A concrete alternative for people who want reliable audio narration of technical material.
items.evidence: Its founder highlighted complex-document accuracy, and another commenter independently called it great.

items.name: Jellypod
items.summary: An AI podcast platform with editable scripts, custom hosts, publishing, and RSS.
items.why_included: It adds editorial control and distribution features beyond simple audio summaries.
items.evidence: A commenter specifically recommended it for script editing, selectable hosts, websites, and RSS publishing.

items.name: Immersive Linear Algebra
items.source_story_title: Immersive Linear Algebra Book with Interactive Figures (2015)
items.summary: A free linear algebra book built around fully interactive visual figures.
items.why_included: A durable, self-contained educational resource rather than a disposable product announcement.
items.evidence: HN surfaced the book for its interactive treatment of vectors, matrices, mappings, and eigenvalues.

items.name: Ente Photos
items.summary: An open-source, end-to-end encrypted, cross-platform photo backup service.
items.why_included: A mature privacy-oriented alternative with hosted and self-hosted paths.
items.evidence: Commenters praised feature parity, desktop apps, continuous exports, and its viability for de-Googling.
